general: The Sheep and the Horse are both compatible zodiac signs, sharing a friendly relationship. Regardless of the overall trend, it's essential to be mentally prepared for changes during this harmonious year. If auspicious events such as marriage, childbirth, property purchase, or starting a business occur during the year, the fortune will be relatively stable. Otherwise, be wary of potentially significant fluctuations. When facing new cooperation opportunities, be cautious of potential risks and adverse outcomes. Although you may feel confident during negotiations, execution may encounter many difficulties. Even simple tasks may present unexpected troubles. While failure may seem inevitable at first, success can ultimately be achieved. Therefore, it's crucial to maintain a calm and balanced perspective on gains and losses. Despite fluctuating fortunes, those born in the Year of the Sheep are fortunate to be blessed by auspicious stars. "The Sun" symbolizes male benefactors; if your target customers are primarily male, such as those selling men's clothing, cars, audio equipment, or models, the Year of the Horse promises abundant wealth. This star also signifies illuminating distant horizons, so business owners may consider expanding into overseas markets. However, the Year of the Horse also brings the inauspicious star "Bad Luck," which will significantly impact interpersonal relationships and may lead to troublesome situations. Fortunately, with the support of "The Sun," challenges can ultimately be overcome smoothly. "Yellow Banner" represents minor troubles within the family, while "Saddle" here applies to interpersonal relationships, potentially leading to a situation where you don't want to offend either side, so careful handling is necessary. In summary, the Year of the Horse brings relatively good fortune. Career-wise, you can expand your network, and new strategies will be effective. However, although the "Sun" star is favorable for action, and the "Saddle" star signifies a transition to a new position, the year is still subject to change, so careful consideration is essential. Especially since the upcoming Year of the Sheep is the Ben Ming Nian (Zodiac Year) for those born under this sign, it is advised that they prepare for the changes of the next two years, particularly those born in spring and summer, who should be extra cautious and plan carefully to ensure a safe passage through the year.

bornYear.reading: Because the annual fortune perfectly matches an individual's Heavenly Stem and Earthly Branch, if there are auspicious events in the family such as purchasing property or the birth of a child, one can proactively take steps to mitigate the negative effects; otherwise, one should pay closer attention to their health. Therefore, it's suggested that men eat vegetarian food on their lunar birthday and, under safe conditions, release animals or celebrate with family.
